bill.short_title: Further Continuing Appropriations Act, 2021, and Other Extensions Act

provisions.description: Extends the continuing resolution by striking the existing date in section 106(3) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2021 and inserting 'December 18, 2020'.

metadata.llm_classification: CR date extension — amends the existing Continuing Appropriations Act, 2021 (P.L. 116-159) to extend the expiration date to December 18, 2020

provisions.raw_text: The Continuing Appropriations Act, 2021 (division A of Public Law 116–159) is amended by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting ''December 18, 2020''.
notes: Division A consists solely of this one-section CR extension
P.L. 116-159 is the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2021, enacted September 30, 2020
Section 106(3) of P.L. 116-159 originally specified a CR end date; this provision replaces that date with December 18, 2020
This effectively extends all FY2021 continuing appropriations at the prior-year rate through the new date

cross_references.target: P.L. 116-159, Division A, Section 106(3)
cross_references.description: Strikes existing CR expiration date and inserts December 18, 2020

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of HHS to provide specified information in response to queries from applicable plans regarding Medicare Secondary Payer determinations, including whether a claimant is or has been entitled to Medicare benefits during the preceding 3 years, and the name and address of any Medicare Advantage or Part D plan in which the claimant is or has been enrolled.

provisions.description: Requires the Attorney General to revise 21 CFR 1306.07(b) within 180 days to allow practitioners to dispense not more than a three-day supply of narcotic drugs to one person at one time for the purpose of initiating maintenance treatment or detoxification treatment (or both) for opioid use disorder.
provisions.title: III

cross_references.description: DEA regulation on dispensing narcotic drugs for maintenance or detoxification treatment

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from statutory PAYGO scorecards (section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010), Senate PAYGO scorecards (H. Con. Res. 71, 115th Congress), and from estimation under the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 and the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
notes: Standard PAYGO exemption provision for health extenders
Subsections (a), (b), and (c) cover statutory PAYGO, Senate PAYGO, and budget scorekeeping classification respectively

cross_references.target: Section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010

Section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985
Section 3(4)(C) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
cross_references.description: Exemption from treatment as appropriation Act

flagged_issues: Division A contains only a single provision extending the CR expiration date; no new dollar amounts are specified
The short title clause at the end of Division A was not extracted per instructions
All provisions are one-week extensions (December 11/12 to December 18/19, 2020) of expiring health programs — part of a short-term CR
SEC. 1303 increases Medicaid Improvement Fund by $18,000,000 (from $3,446,000,000 to $3,464,000,000) as a budgetary offset
SEC. 1301 and SEC. 1302 are policy changes scored as budgetary offsets under Title III
No new discretionary budget authority in Division B; all spending is mandatory
